verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It possesses a signal peptide and is constitutively secreted into the extracellular milieu (seminal plasma, serum) without a transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. It is not stably anchored to the cell surface — it exists in equilibrium as a soluble secreted enzyme. While it is elevated in prostate cancer and used as a biomarker in fluids, there is no documented stable TM-partner tethering or wash-resistant surface pool. Verdict is no / secreted_only.
